audit_items.proof_trace: In find_connection (L694-696), lcu = lc->lconn_server is immediately dereferenced via lcu->lud_scheme and lcu->lud_port with no null guard. A connection with lconn_server == NULL can enter ld->ld_conns when ldap_new_connection is called with connect=0 (open.c L351, L586 for pre-existing-socket paths); LDAP_CALLOC zero-initializes lconn_server and it is never assigned when connect==0 with url==NULL. The contrast is direct: ldap_dump_connection (L852) guards if (lc->lconn_server != NULL) before the same field, proving the developer knows it can be NULL, yet find_connection omits this guard.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A client or proxy using ldap_initialize_fd(fd, LDAP_PROTO_TCP, NULL) (no URL) establishes a connection with lconn_server=NULL in ld->ld_conns; when a malicious upstream LDAP server sends a referral response, ldap_chase_v3referrals calls find_connection which dereferences lcu->lud_scheme with lcu==NULL, causing a SIGSEGV crash.
